title: Zhuangzi, Chapter 17: Autumn Floods
description: Chapter 17 of the Zhuangzi — the Taoist masterpiece of paradox and parable.
introduction: Chapter 17 of the Zhuangzi — the Taoist masterpiece of paradox and parable.

translation.text: The time of the autumn floods came and the hundred streams poured into the Yellow River. Its racing current swelled to such proportions that, looking from bank to bank, it was impossible to distinguish a cow from a horse. Then the Lord of the River was beside himself with joy, believing that all the beauty in the world belonged to him alone.
